exemplar.title: Modelling the surface area of a hill

detailedExemplarData.title: Modelling the surface area of a hill

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear separation into Method #1 and Method #2 with headings enhances organization and logical flow
Logical development from personal motivation through modelling methods to evaluation
Effective use of numbered sections, tables, figures and captions to guide the reader
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and lengthy algebraic substitutions interrupt conciseness
Repetitive sentences could be condensed for improved relevance
Minor layout issues (uneven spacing, mis-labelled figures) detract slightly from readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a coherent and logically developed structure with clear headings and a sensible sequence of sections, fitting the band-3 descriptor. While most content is relevant and organized, several pages reproduce familiar algebraic steps and minor layout issues prevent it from reaching the highest standard of conciseness and exceptional organization. Thus, a score of 3 out of 4 reflects solid performance with targeted areas for tightening and polishing.

strengths: Effective integration of equations, GeoGebra screenshots, labelled photos and tables to clarify the modelling process
Mostly consistent and accurate use of mathematical language and notation throughout
Multiple representations are used effectively to support understanding
weaknesses: Explanatory prose is sometimes sparse, leading to occasional jumps in logic
Minor notation slips (e.g. missing differentials, inconsistent subscripts) occur
Some dense algebraic blocks could be split for improved clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is generally strong, with effective use of multiple representations and mostly consistent notation supporting the exploration. However, sparse explanatory prose and occasional logical leaps keep it from the top band of clarity. Awarding 3 out of 4 reflects the effective integration of representations balanced against areas needing more detailed explanation and polish.

strengths: Clear personal motivation rooted in childhood experience and community planning context
Use of GeoGebra sketches and original measurements demonstrates initiative
Evidence of independent thinking in selecting and comparing two modelling strategies
weaknesses: Creative leaps remain modest and reliant on standard textbook techniques
Some personal choices (e.g. contour-point selection) could be justified more explicitly
Innovation is consistent but not yet outstanding throughout
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows significant evidence of independent thinking and a clear personal approach through original measurements and technology use, meeting band-2. However, creativity and methodological innovation do not reach the highest level of originality or sophistication. A score of 2 out of 3 accurately captures the solid engagement with room to deepen personal innovation.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on modelling assumptions, measurement limitations and accuracy in the evaluation section
Clear evaluation of outcomes and discussion of implications for real-life applications
Acknowledgement of sources of error and suggestions for future improvement
weaknesses: Critical analysis of why errors arise lacks depth and is not woven throughout the report
Reflection is concentrated mainly in the final section rather than integrated
Limited exploration of how modelling choices could be refined
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and a clear evaluation of outcomes, aligning with band-2 descriptors. However, analysis of the sources and impact of errors is relatively basic and confined to a single section, preventing the work from achieving deeper critical reflection. A mark of 2 out of 3 balances clear evaluative insight against the need for more thorough, sustained reflection.

strengths: Relevant SL-level mathematics (geometry, algebraic modelling, numerical integration, surface-area formulae) are applied appropriately
Good understanding demonstrated through systematic derivations of linear and quadratic functions
Procedures are mostly correct, showing comprehension of course-level techniques
weaknesses: Minor computational slips and rounding inconsistencies introduce small inaccuracies
Modelling assumptions (e.g. half-ellipsoid shapes) are not always justified in the text
A typographical error and unit inconsistency in one calculation suggest need for careful checking
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration employs a range of SL-appropriate mathematical methods effectively and demonstrates good understanding with mostly correct procedures, fitting band-4. Small computational errors and unsubstantiated modelling assumptions prevent it from reaching the highest bands. Therefore, a score of 4 out of 6 accurately reflects solid mathematical use with targeted improvements needed for precision and justification.

strandSelections.reasoning: While the algebraic derivations are set out in order, explanatory prose is sometimes sparse, and jumps in logic (e.g. sudden choice of half-ellipses for the unfolded model) reduce clarity.  Hence the work shows âsome logical structure and clarityâ but not the consistent strength of the higher band.

Derivatives, integrals and surface-area calculations are generally correct; mis-typing of one Pythagorean result and rounding inconsistencies create minor inaccuracies.  Overall understanding is good and procedures are mostly correct, fitting the band-4 description of âgood understanding and mostly correctâ.

The exploration contains equations, GeoGebra screenshots, labelled photos and tables that together clarify the modelling process.  These representations are integrated effectively to support understanding, satisfying the âeffective useâ description.

The student leads the reader from a personal motivation, through two distinct modelling methods, and finally to an evaluation.  Headings and sub-headings create a clear sequential pathway and each section builds reasonably on the previous one.  Occasional digressions (e.g. lengthy algebraic substitutions that do not inform later reasoning) interrupt the flow, so the development is sound rather than exemplary, fitting the âcoherent and logically developedâ descriptor.

Using two methods and GeoGebra shows some initiative, but creative leaps are modest and largely follow textbook techniques.  Evidence therefore matches the âsome creativity or initiative shownâ description.

The student notes a 7 mÂ² difference between methods and lists several sources of error, but there is little depth in analysing why those errors arise or how serious they are.  This constitutes only âbasic analysis of resultsâ.

Limitations are acknowledged and linked to possible over- or under-estimation of area; the student comments on the usefulness of real-life scaling.  While suggestions for future improvement are brief, there is a âclear evaluation of outcomes and implicationsâ.

The evaluation section comments on accuracy, modelling assumptions and measurement limitations, amounting to âmeaningful reflectionâ.  However, reflection is confined mainly to the final section rather than woven throughout, so it does not reach the highest band.

Variables, function notation, derivatives and integral symbols are generally applied correctly and consistently.  Only minor slips (e.g. missing differentials, inconsistent sub-scripts) occur, so usage is âmostly consistentâ.

The narrative repeatedly links the mathematics to the studentâs childhood experience and community planning issue, demonstrating a clear personal approach, yet innovation is limited, so the strand sits comfortably in band 2.

Most content is relevant to determining the surface area, yet several pages reproduce elementary algebra steps or repeat information already visible on screenshots.  These additions lengthen the report without deepening insight, so the work is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ rather than consistently concise.

Geometry, algebraic modelling, numerical integration and surface-area formulae are all pertinent to the problem and are applied in both methods.  Despite a few computational slips, the mathematics is largely effective and well matched to the task, meriting band 4.

Selecting a personally significant hill, designing two self-chosen modelling strategies and collecting original measurements illustrates significant independent thinking, but methodological choices are fairly standard within IA contexts, aligning with band 2.

Numbered sections, tables, figures and captions demonstrate deliberate structure (introduction â exploration â evaluation â bibliography).  Images are correctly referenced and placed close to the text they support, enabling smooth navigation.  Minor layout issues (uneven spacing, mis-labelled figure numbers) prevent the piece from reaching the âexceptionally well organisedâ standard.

Pythagoras, quadratic fitting and arc-length integration are solidly within SL level and employed correctly.  The mixture of algebraic and numerical techniques shows the mathematics is âfully commensurate with course levelâ, though not yet demonstrating the sophistication expected for the top two bands.

annotations.comment: Rounding 1.5056746 m to 1.51 m introduces minor inconsistency with later precision; consider retaining extra digits until final result.
Present a concise list or mini-table summarizing which f_i(x) are linear vs quadratic to improve clarity of multiple representations.
The introduction effectively ties personal childhood experiences to a community planning context, demonstrating clear personal engagement and relevance to the research question.
Sentence about choosing front and side pictures is lengthy and slightly repetitive; consider condensing to improve conciseness and relevance.
Consider adding a brief overview of both modelling methods at the end of the introduction to guide the reader through the reportâs structure.
The calculation âx = 1426253484 â 143 mâ contains a clear typo and unit inconsistency; re-derive this step to ensure accuracy.
In the Pythagorean theorem explanation, include the differential element (e.g. âdxâ) consistently to adhere to proper mathematical notation.
Figure 2 is clearly labelled and supports the Pythagorean measurement description effectively, enhancing clarity of the method.
Multiple representations (equations, figures, tables) are integrated effectively in the ellipsoid surface area calculation, showing strong mathematical communication.
The exponent 1.6075 in the ellipsoid formula is used without justification; briefly explain its origin (e.g. Knud Thomsenâs approximation) to aid understanding.
The clear separation into Method #1 and Method #2 with headings enhances organization and logical flow in the exploration.
The assumption of half-ellipsoids is not justified in text; add a brief rationale for why each peak models a semi-ellipsoid.
Introduce a transition sentence at the start of Method #2 to link it logically from the previous section and guide the reader.
The GeoGebra sketch in Figure 3 demonstrates initiative and creativity in using technology to model the hillâs contour.
The sentence listing assumed parabolas and linear functions misnames f2(x) twice; revise for accuracy and avoid confusion.
Table 1 compiles coordinates clearly, aiding reproducibility and demonstrating thorough data collection.
The table columns are somewhat narrow, causing cramped text; adjust column widths or font size for improved readability.
Annotate why you selected each contour point in Table 1 to deepen personal engagement and justify model choices.
The step-by-step derivation of the linear function y=fâ(x) displays systematic use of algebra and demonstrates solid understanding.
The algebraic solution for fâ(x) is thorough, but intermediate sub-steps are dense; consider splitting into smaller blocks for clarity.
The final coefficients for y=fâ(x) accurately reflect the SL-level quadratic modelling, showing good understanding and application.
State the domain [0.1742; 1.8842] in parentheses or prose instead of inline to avoid misinterpretation of the function's applicability.
Side-view placement in Cartesian axes and subsequent point selection shows outstanding initiative in capturing 3D geometry via 2D projections.
For fâ(x), the constant term c=8.0822 appears large given point coordinates; recheck substitution to confirm accuracy.
Include the x-interval explicitly when introducing the arc length integral to connect the formula directly to your functionsâ domains.
